title: _threadmodule: repr() of a shared RLock reads the owner thread-id non-atomically while another thread acquires/releases it (rlock_repr)
description: rlock_repr reads self->lock.thread (the recursive mutex owner) with a plain load, but acquire()/release() store it via _Py_atomic_store_ullong_relaxed (lock.c:439/466). repr() of a shared RLock races with acquire/release on a public, explicitly-shareable API.
sites: Modules/_threadmodule.c rlock_repr:1291 (PLAIN read of self->lock.thread -- the racing read)
Modules/_threadmodule.c rlock_repr:1295 (PLAIN read of self->lock.level -- secondary racy field)
Python/lock.c _PyRecursiveMutex_LockTimed:439 (atomic store m->thread = tid on acquire)
Include/internal/pycore_lock.h:159 (_PyRecursiveMutex { PyMutex mutex; unsigned long long thread; size_t level; })
race_kind: non-atomic read of an atomically-written field (owner thread-id) in a read-only-looking accessor (tp_repr)
severity: low (value-benign, crash-free; single aligned 8-byte word, no torn read; repr may print a stale owner/count; strictly UB: plain read vs atomic write)
suggested_fix: make rlock_repr's reads atomic to match the writers: owner = _Py_atomic_load_ullong_relaxed(&self->lock.thread) and _Py_atomic_load_size_relaxed(&self->lock.level); relaxed ordering suffices (informational, single word)
framework_noise_assessment: REAL user-facing bug, NOT framework noise. The fleet deduper labeled the vehicle 'tsanFRAME' because the frames are in _threadmodule.c, but rlock_repr is the tp_repr slot of the public _thread.RLock type (== threading.RLock()). repr() is ordinary read-only user code (logging/debugging/%r), RLock is designed for cross-thread sharing, and the race is on the RLock's own owner field -- exactly what a real program with a shared lock + a logging thread hits. The mutating side already uses relaxed atomics; only the repr() reader was left non-atomic.
notes: Structurally mirrors TSAN-0005 (dec_hash): a read-only-looking C accessor reads a field written atomically elsewhere. self->lock.level (:1295) is a secondary racy field (mutated by the owner under the mutex, read by repr without it) and should be fixed in the same change. _thread_RLock__recursion_count_impl (:1250) reads level only under _PyRecursiveMutex_IsLockedByCurrentThread (owner-only), so it is NOT part of this race. Audit _threadmodule.c for other plain reads of _PyRecursiveMutex/PyMutex internals in non-locking accessors.

related_issues.note: Data race in repr() of _thread.RLock -- exactly this (PR #153299, both CLOSED/fixed). Our build predates the fix.

assessment: Real user-facing race (repr reads self->lock.thread plainly vs atomic writer). ALREADY REPORTED+FIXED #153292/PR#153299. NOTE: fusil deduper mislabeled the vehicle tsanFRAME (FRAMEWORK_FILES regex matches _threadmodule.c) though rlock_repr is public tp_repr -- a deduper over-suppression risk to fix in fusil.
